question: Describe in detail how The Beast was secured and provided shelter in the backyard.
answer: The Beast was secured by fastening a heavy chain to an I-Beam girder that had been driven into the ground and cemented in place. The chain was attached to a collar around The Beast's neck, anchoring it to the girder. For shelter, Mr. Mertle built a large lean-to using old bullet-holed tin advertising signs, which The Beast then moved under and rested beneath, causing dust to rise from the ground due to its weight.
related_context: Construction workers drove an I-Beam girder into the ground... filled the surrounding chasm with wet cement... A welder later attached a heavy chain to the girder... Mr. Mertle takes the other end of the chain and fits it around The Beast's neck like a collar... Mr. Mertle assembled a large lean-to in the backyard using old bullet-holed tin advertising signs... The Beast moved beneath the shelter and lay down, the force of its body hitting the ground sending a burst of dust flying from underneath the structure.
